1  3-22/6/97 Version No. 001 
Egg Industry (Deregulation) Act 1993 
Act No. 47/1993 
Version  as at 1 July 1997 
 
The Parliament of Victoria enacts as follows: 
1. Purpose 
The purposes of this Act are to deregulate the egg 
industry, to repeal the Egg Industry Act 1989 , to 
transfer the property, rights and liabilities of the Victorian Egg Marketing Board to the Egg Industry Co-operative Limited and to make various other provisions with respect to the egg industry. 
2. Commencement 
 (1) Section 1 and this section come into operation on 
the day on which this Act receives the Royal Assent. 
 (2) The remaining provisions of this Act come into 
operation on a day or days to be proclaimed. 
 (3) If a provision referred to in sub-section (2) does 
not come into operation within a period of 6 months beginning on and including the day on which this Act receives the Royal Assent, it comes into operation on the first day after the end of that period. 
3. Definitions 
In this Act— 
"appointed day"  means the day on which section 
4 comes into operation; 
 
 Act No. 47/1993  
   
Egg Industry (Deregulation) Act 1993 
2 
 3-22/6/97 "Board"  means the Victorian Egg Marketing 
Board established under the old Act; 
"Co-operative"  means the Egg Industry Co-
operative Limited incorporated under the 
Co-operation Act 1981  on 17 February 
1993; 
"liabilities"  means all liabilities, duties and 
obligations, whether actual, contingent or prospective; 
"old Act"  means the Egg Industry Act 1989 as 
in force immediately before the appointed day; 
"rights"  means all rights, powers, privileges and 
immunities, whether actual, contingent or prospective. 
4. Repeal of Egg Industry Act 
The Egg Industry Act 1989  is repealed . 
5. Transfer of property, rights and liabilities 
 (1) On the appointed day— 
(a) the property and rights of the Board vest in 
the Co-operative; and 
(b) the liabilities of the Board become the 
liabilities of the Co-operative; and 
(c) the Co-operative becomes the successor in 
law of the Board; and 
(d) the Board is dissolved. 
6. Issue of shares 
 (1) Fully paid shares in the Co-operative of $0.50 
each are deemed to have been issued by the Co-operative on the appointed day to a total nominal value equal to the value declared by Order of the Governor in Council under section 8. s. 4 
 
 Act No. 47/1993  
   
Egg Industry (Deregulation) Act 1993 
3 
 3-22/6/97  (2) The shares referred to in sub-section (1) are 
deemed to have been allotted to eligible holders of hen quota on the appointed day, and each eligible holder of hen quota is deemed to have been allotted a whole number of shares equal to the number or closest whole number  determined  in  accordance with the 
 formula  V 
x Q  where— 
  $0.50  T  
(a) V is the total nominal value of the shares 
deemed to have been issued under sub-section (1); and 
(b) Q is the hen quota to which the eligible 
holder was entitled as at the day before the appointed day under the old Act; and 
(c) T is the total issued quota under the old Act 
as at the day before the appointed day. 
 (3) If an eligible holder of hen quota owed money to 
the Board as at the day before the appointed day, for the purposes of sub-section (2), the number of shares deemed to have been allotted to the eligible holder is reduced by a number equal to twice the amount (in whole dollars) of the money so owed and that number of shares shall be deemed to be forfeited. 
 (4) A share forfeited by reason of sub-section (3) may 
be dealt with in accordance with the rules of the Co-operative. 
 (5) In this section— 
"eligible holder of hen quota"  means a person 
who was entitled to hen quota under the old Act as at the day before the appointed day. s. 6 
 
 Act No. 47/1993  
   
Egg Industry (Deregulation) Act 1993 
4 
 3-22/6/97 7. Co-operative to issue documents, amend records 
For the purposes of giving effect to this Act, the 
Co-operative must issue such share certificates or other documents as are necessary, and amend its records accordingly. 
8. Value of property, rights and liabilities 
The value of the property and rights of the Board, 
less the liabilities of the Board on the day before the appointed day is the value declared by Order of the Governor in Council on the recommendation of the Minister after consulting with the Valuer General appointed under the 
Valuation of Land Act 1960 . 
9. Membership of the Co-operative 
A person to whom shares are deemed to have been 
allotted under this Act is deemed to have become a member of the Co-operative in accordance with 
the provisions of the Co-operation Act 1981  and 
the rules of the Co-operative. 
10. Payment in lieu of stamp duty etc. 
The Board must, before the appointed day, pay  
to the Consolidated Fund the sum of $567 000  as payment in lieu of stamp duty and other charges. 
11. Audited report 
 (1) A person appointed by the Minister shall prepare 
financial statements of the Board and the Committee as referred to in section 47(4) of the old Act for the period beginning on 1 July 1992 and ending on the day before the appointed day. 
 (2) The financial statements prepared under this 
section must be audited by the Auditor-General who has in respect of that audit, all the powers s. 7 
 
 Act No. 47/1993  
   
Egg Industry (Deregulation) Act 1993 
5 
 3-22/6/97 conferred on the Auditor-General by any law 
relating to the audit of the public accounts. 
 (3) The person appointed for the purposes of sub-
section (1) and the Auditor-General, and each officer of that person or the Auditor-General have right of access at all times to the books of the Board and the Committee held by the Co-operative. 
 (4) The person appointed for the purposes of sub-
section (1) must submit the audited report to the Minister within 3 months (or such longer period as the Minister permits) after being so appointed. 
 (5) The Co-operative must pay to the Consolidated 
Fund amounts to be determined by the Minister and the Auditor-General respectively to defray the costs and expenses of the report and the audit under this section. 
 (6) In this section— 
"Committee"  means the Egg Industry Licensing 
Committee established under the old Act.

 Act No. 47/1993  
   
Egg Industry (Deregulation) Act 1993 
6 
 3-22/6/97 NOTES 
1. General Information 
Minister's second reading speech — 
Legislative Assembly: 29 April 1993  
Legislative Council: 12 May 1993  
The long title for the Bill for this Ac t was "A Bill to deregulate the egg 
industry, to repeal the Egg Industry Act 1989 , to transfer the property, 
rights and liabilities of the Victorian Egg Marketing Board to the Egg Industry Co-operative Limited and to make various other provisions with 
respect to the egg industry.". 
The Egg Industry (Deregulation) Act 1993  was assented to on 1 June 1993 
and came into operation as follows: 
Ss. 1 and 2 on 1 June 1993: s. 2(1) Ss. 3 and 10 on 10 June 1993; ss. 4 to 9 and s.11 on 12 June 1993: 
Government Gazette: 10 June 1993 p.1478. Notes 
 
 Act No. 47/1993

 3-22/6/97 2. Table of Amendments 
There are no amendments made to the Egg Industry (Deregulation) Act 
1993  by Acts and subordinate instruments. Notes
